So you've got this far all the way to the sale and then the person tells you that they're not the decision maker. This is a lesson right here that shows the beginning of the sales process. You need to identify that the person you're speaking to is what's known as the decision maker. Make sure literally with a qualifying question at the very beginning of the sale after you meet them, hey, after I present all this value to you and after we see if we can put some kind of deal together, are you the person who's going to be making the decision in order for us to come into your business and help you out? That is a way for that person to say, oh yes I am, I'm the CEO. Or that's a way for the person to say, oh no, I actually need my three other partners to come in. Oh that's great, who are your three other partners names? As soon as you identify those other decision makers, collect the data, put it in your CRM or put it in your Excel sheet or write down on a sticky pad and put it next to your computer screen that that person has other people associated with your ability to get revenue from that organization. You need to go out of your way to make sure that when you are selling people, once again, you ask a qualifying question at the beginning, if you get all the way to the end though and you hear at the end, well Jeremy, I'd love to bring you into our business today and I'd love for you to execute that for us but I need to go talk to my boss in order to make that decision. Womp, womp, immediately once again if you identify that you've gotten this far and you've not had the decision maker on the line or in front of you the entire time, you need to get that person in there and you have to realize you're going to have to resell everything again to that other person in a summarized way. Commonly, the decision maker will place somebody below them on the phone with you or they'll be on the phone and won't be saying anything, you have no idea and they'll be listening to see how you sell what you're selling, how you treat that employee and if you're able to identify that they aren't the decision maker or not. If you get all the way to the end, the person who is not the decision maker, they are called the gatekeeper and the gatekeeper is somebody that you need to treat like a god or a goddess. You typically want to buy them gifts or fluff them up but not in a posturing way in a very authentic, genuine, real way. You have to understand that that person is the person holding you back from making money off of that organization. You need to understand that the gatekeeper needs to have a positive relationship with you and your company and they need to be warmed up if you will and educated enough to sound smart to the person they're going to bring the deal to. That is the key with understanding gatekeepers. They need to be educated, feel confident and smart and like they have a reason to deliver value to their boss. You see, the gatekeeper wants to bring the silver platter piece of value to their boss. They want to bring your deal on a plate and be like, hey boss, look what I found in the market for us. It's this incredible thing that will help our business a ton. Look and here's everything about it that I've learned and I'm super smart. Look at me and then they want it. That boss will be like, oh wow, thank you so much. I appreciate it. Get them on the phone. Let's bring them in. But you have to educate the gatekeeper, make them feel smart. But first identify if you have the decision maker not at the beginning. And if you found yourself at the end, turn them into an advocate, turn them into a messenger, educate your messenger and get the decision maker on the phone. Or be prepared to have to resell. I'll see you in the next video.
